howItWorks.intro: This package insurance calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: UPS/FedEx: ~$2.50 per $100 of declared value (first $100 free)
USPS: tiered rates starting at $2.45 for up to $50
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Is shipping insurance worth it?
faq.answer: For items over $100, yes. Carrier liability for uninsured packages is typically limited to $100 (UPS/FedEx) or $0 (USPS). The cost of insurance (1-3% of value) is cheap compared to the risk of loss or damage.

faq.question: What does shipping insurance cover?
faq.answer: Shipping insurance covers loss, theft, and damage during transit. It does not cover items improperly packaged, perishable goods, or prohibited items. Claims require proof of value and documentation of damage.

faq.question: Are third-party insurers cheaper?
faq.answer: Yes, typically 40-60% cheaper than carrier-declared value. Companies like Shipsurance, U-PIC, and Parcel Insurance Plan offer rates around $1-1.50 per $100 vs $2.50 at UPS/FedEx. Claims processing may take longer.
